title: Vagh Rodiek

The '''Vagh Rodiek''' ([[Yuuzhan Vong (language)|Yuuzhan Vong]] for "'''Rodian Slaves'''") were the offspring of the dreadful [[Yuuzhan Vong]] bioengineering and the result of genetic manipulation, vivisection, and brutal selective breeding. At the beginning of their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|conquest campaign]], the race of invaders, the Yuuzhan Vong, [[Battle of Rodia (Yuuzhan Vong War)|subjugated]] the planet [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]]. The native [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]] were chosen to be radically altered and transformed into a new kind of mindless [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] soldiers, like the [[Chazrach]] before them.

==Appearance and biology==
The former Rodians had been perverted beyond recognition and had lost the slightest trace of reason. The Yuuzhan Vong shapers merged Rodian biological material with parts of other creatures.<ref name="GS" /> The result of this horrific experiment was the creation of crab-like monstrosities known as Vagh Rodiek. The creatures moved on six<ref name="CE" /> or four pointed legs resembling those of arthropods; their thin arms had been replaced with [[Meter/Legends|half-meter]] bone hooks, and their small heads, the only feature that could reveal the terrible story of their creation, were now crowned with a sharpened crest of ossified bristles.<ref name="IYV" />

The slave-soldiers, created to be obedient and silent, were destined to go to the battlefields soon after their birth. The Vagh Rodiek fought under Yuuzhan Vong command alongside the [[Chazrach]], a race of [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] servitors who had shared a fate similar to that of the Rodians. The disfigured Rodians had lost the gift of speech; however, their new masters had endowed them with an understanding of the [[Yuuzhan Vong (language)|own language]].<ref name="IYV" />

==History==
===Rodia project===
{{Quote|My homeworld is about to suffer the most terrible depredations—|The rules of Rodia, [[Navik/Legends|Navik the Red]]|<ref name="BP">''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]''</ref>}}
In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] race of invaders, the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], launched a massive [[Yuuzhan Vong War|conquest campaign]] directed against all worlds of the [[Galactic community]].<ref name="VP">''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]''</ref> [[26 ABY/Legends|Less than a year later]]<ref name="IYV" /> after the beginning of the war, as part of the campaign to divide the [[Corellian Run/Legends|Corellian Run]], the Yuuzhan Vong [[Battle of Rodia (Yuuzhan Vong War)|invaded]] the planet [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]].<ref name="EA" /> With the world broken, the invaders were free to do whatever they pleased: the planet’s polluted cities were obliterated with metal-eating microbes, and [[damutek]] laboratories were planted in the open [[Jungle/Legends|jungles]].<ref name="IYV" />

[[File:MR T.Lah.jpg|thumb|left|240px|The Warmaster Tsavong Lah who ordered the experiments on the Rodians to begin]]
Although the Yuuzhan Vong occupied their homeworld, many Rodian fighters continued to put up fierce resistance from the dense [[Forest/Legends|forests]]. The invaders developed a grudging respect for the violence-loving Rodians. This opinion became so common that some believed the Rodians might be able to assume a role within Yuuzhan Vong society as favored slaves – once they had been subdued and properly shaped.<ref name="NJOS">''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]''</ref> The [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] personally ordered that the [[Terraforming/Legends|reshaping]] of the planet should not be limited to its environment but should also focus on the native [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]]. The [[Master Shaper]] [[Taug Molou]] was instructed to lead the Rodia project and to breed a new generation of tractable [[Slavery/Legends|slave]]-soldiers. However, Molou did not merely wish to control the Rodians through [[Yorik-Kul|coral implants]] – he sought to create an completely new biological species and was not afraid to violate [[Shaping protocol|religious protocols]] to achieve it. Thousands of Rodians [[Death/Legends|died]] in vivisection chambers before the first Vagh Rodiek emerged from its [[birthing blister]].<ref name="IYV" />

===Further fate===
The new race of slaves, little more than animals without the guidance of their Yuuzhan Vong masters, joined the ranks of the countless conquering horde. Nevertheless, Molou's creations found little practical use until the [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|fall of Coruscant]]. The Vagh Rodiek were often made available to military commanders, particularly those operating near [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]], where they replaced the Chazrach. A typical Vagh Rodiek strike unit consisted of three to twelve individuals.<ref name="IYV" /> Presumably, the Vagh Rodiek, like other Yuuzhan Vong bioengineered creatures, were destroyed under the terms of the [[Sekot accords|surrender]]<ref name="UP">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref> or, just as likely, became part of the Yuuzhan Vong reshaped biosphere of Rodia.<ref name="LE">''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The Vagh Rodiek are awful, crab-like monstrosities created by Yuuzhan Vong shapers through the mutation of Rodian slaves. I created these things for the article "Being Yuuzhan Vong" (illustrated by Anthony Waters) in an issue of Star Wars Gamer, and I love referencing them when I can.|Daniel Wallace's comment|<ref name="Blog">https://web.archive.org/web/20100326071707/https://blogs.starwars.com/danwallace/145</ref>}}
The Vagh Rodiek first appeared in [[Daniel Wallace]]’s article "[[I, Yuuzhan Vong]]" in ''[[Star Wars Gamer 8]]'' {{C|[[2002]]}}. The creature was illustrated by artist [[Anthony Waters]]. Later, while working on ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' {{C|[[2009]]}}, Wallace added a mention of the Vagh Rodiek to the entry about Rodia.
